# UC-17.1.76 · ISE Group-Based Policy (GBP / Microsegmentation) Effective-Policy Drift

> **Criticality:** Medium &middot; **Difficulty:** Advanced &middot; **Pillar:** Security &middot; **Type:** Compliance, Operations &middot; **Wave:** Run &middot; **Status:** Verified

*The microsegmentation matrix is a big spreadsheet of which device groups can talk to which. We check it daily against the approved version and flag any cell that has changed.*

---

## Description

Detects unauthorised changes to the SGT-to-SGT effective policy matrix. Compares each cell of the current matrix against the approved baseline and flags any drift.

## Value

The GBP matrix is the heart of microsegmentation policy. A single cell change (e.g., `Employees → DC_PCI` from deny to allow) can compromise an entire segmentation strategy. Drift detection catches unauthorized changes within the polling interval.

## Implementation

Daily. Requires a curated baseline CSV checked into version control.

## Control test

### Positive scenario

Modify a single SGT-matrix cell. UC-17.1.76 detects drift on next run.

### Negative scenario

Matrix matches baseline. No drift.

## Detailed Implementation

Standard pattern.

## SPL

```spl
| inputlookup ise_sgt_matrix.csv 
| join type=outer source_sgt destination_sgt [| inputlookup ise_sgt_matrix_baseline.csv | rename action as baseline_action]
| eval drift=if(action!=baseline_action, "DRIFTED", "ok")
| where drift=="DRIFTED"
```

## Visualization

Drift cells over time; current matrix vs. baseline visualization.

## Known False Positives

- Approved baseline change pending update — coordinate with policy engineering.

## References

- [Cisco ISE 3.4 — TrustSec Policy Matrix](https://www.cisco.com/c/en/us/)
